params.prompt:
Dramatic, realistic editorial still-life photograph, full color (NOT grayscale/duotone), ink-black (#101010) background, vertical canvas. Extremely minimalist composition, no people, no barbershop interior, no furniture, no screens or TVs of any kind: on the far left edge of the frame, a large Spain national flag (horizontal bands: red, yellow, red) hangs in heavy realistic draped fabric with deep folds, lit dramatically from the side by a single warm gold key light that catches the fabric texture and casts long shadows. On the far right edge, mirrored, a Portugal national flag (vertical green and red bands, simple color blocking, no readable coat-of-arms detail) hangs in the same dramatic realistic fabric-fold style with the same gold rim light. Both flags fade into pure black toward the center of the frame, leaving the entire center two-thirds of the canvas as clean empty ink-black negative space reserved for text. Moody, cinematic, high-end studio lighting, sharp fabric texture, shallow depth of field at the edges.

Graphic overlay on top, following the brand social-post-frame template exactly:
- Top-left: a solid Nordic Yellow (#FFD200) pill/eyebrow tag with small bold uppercase Fira Sans Condensed text reading "HOY EN NORDIC"
- Center of the empty negative space: one bold display headline set in the exact hand-cut marker letterform style shown in the attached "Lost in South" character-map reference image, all uppercase, tight line-height, reading "ESPAÑA - PORTUGAL A LA 1PM" in bone-white (#F7F4EC), with the word "1PM" recolored in Nordic Yellow (#FFD200) as the single highlight word — no other yellow text.
- Small caption line below the headline in Fira Sans Condensed regular, bone-white at reduced opacity, reading "Agenda tu servicio y sigue el partido con nosotros."
- Bottom corner: small watermark of the exact logo shown in the attached logo reference image (white Viking-helmet mark + NORDIC / Barber Shop wordmark on transparent/ink background) — reproduce it faithfully, do not redesign it.

Keep the yellow accent to roughly 10-15% of the frame (pill + one highlight word only). Do not add any other colors to the graphic overlay itself — the two flags are the only additional color in the image.
